author: doll-agent
content: Reject empty separator in split() and split_ragged() to match numpy's ValueError behavior. Rust's str::split('') returns the surprising ['', 'a', 'b', 'c', ''] pattern (one empty token around each char boundary). Add validate_separator helper that returns FerrayError::InvalidValue for empty input. 4 tests: empty separator errs in split, errs in split_ragged, single-char separator still works, multi-char separator splits on the full sequence.
